title: GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation
abstract: GEM (Geometric Entropy Mixing) is a framework for LLM data curation that reformulates semantic corpus categorization as entropy-regularized clustering on the hypersphere with a mixing-balance regularizer. It uses a von Mises-Fisher mixture model, MM-based inference, GIS-based representative sampling, and teacher-student distillation to produce scalable, interpretable, and balanced taxonomies for downstream data mixing. Experiments with 1.1B-parameter models show consistent gains when GEM taxonomies are integrated with DoReMi, Perf, and RegMix.
publication_status: TeX source uses icml2026 accepted style; formal proceedings metadata not independently verified
